title: 10 best herbal tinctures for tinnitus
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for tinnitus are liquid extracts derived from plants that help alleviate symptoms of tinnitus, a condition characterized by ringing, buzzing, or other sounds in the ears. They work by improving blood flow, reducing inflammation, and promoting relaxation. Some of the best herbal tinctures for tinnitus include Ginkgo biloba, Zingiber officinale, Avena sativa, Melissa officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, Bacopa monnieri, Hypericum perforatum, Scutellaria baicalensis, Passiflora incarnata, and Angelica sinensis. If left untreated, tinnitus can lead to anxiety, depression, sleep disorders, and even social isolation, severely impacting a person's quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with tinnitus because of its unique composition, which includes flavonoids and terpenes that have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the inner ear, which can contribute to the development and exacerbation of tinnitus. The tincture's active ingredients also have been shown to improve blood flow and circulation to the ears, allowing for better communication between the auditory nerve and the brain and potentially reducing tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the inner ear, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms.
Vasodilatory properties: The terpenes in Ginkgo biloba dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow to the ears and potentially reducing pressure and noise-induced damage that can cause tinnitus.

Neuroprotective effects: Ginkgo biloba's flavonoids have been shown to protect against neurotoxicity and promote neuronal health, which may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms by reducing noise sensitivity and improving auditory processing.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These polyphenolic compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the inner ear, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms.
Bilobalide: As a triterpene lignan, bilobalide has neuroprotective properties, which may help repair or maintain the integrity of auditory nerve cells and reduce tinnitus symptoms.
Quercetin: This flavonoid acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the inner ear and auditory nerve, which can contribute to tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary part used due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.

Seeds: The seeds are used due to their unique combination of flavonoids and terpenoids, as well as other bioactive compounds, which may help alleviate inflammation and oxidative stress associated with tinnitus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50-75 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer or early fall for optimal potency.
Chop the ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces to increase the surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped ginkgo biloba leaves with 500 milliliters of 80-90 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks to allow for extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort tinctures helps with tinnitus because it has been shown to reduce inflammation in the body, which is a common underlying cause of this condition. The herb also exhibits antioxidant properties, which can help protect the delicate hair cells in the inner ear from damage. Additionally, St John's wort has been found to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which may help alleviate the psychological distress associated with tinnitus. By addressing these underlying factors, herbal St John's wort tinctures may provide relief from tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: St John's Wort contains antioxidants that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the ear, which may contribute to the development and progression of tinnitus.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The flavonoids present in St John's Wort have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the ear, potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.

Neuroprotective compounds: St John's Wort contains neuroprotective compounds like hyperforin, which may help protect the inner ear nerves from damage and improve communication between them, potentially reducing tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: This prenylated phenolic compound has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate tinnitus by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the inner ear.

Naphthodianthrones (e.g., hypericin): These compounds have been found to have neuroprotective effects, which can help protect the inner ear from damage and promote the repair of hair cells, potentially reducing tinnitus symptoms.

Flavonoids (e.g., quercetin): These polyphenolic compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the inner ear, potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain hyperoside, a flavonoid glycoside that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Flowers: They contain hyperforin and hypericin, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe and protect the auditory system.
Roots: They contain hypericin and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the auditory system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of dried aerial parts of hypericum perforatum in the flowering stage at dawn when dew is still present.
Combine the harvested plant material with 500ml of 40% ethanol or glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and let it steep for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar occasionally.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to small amber glass bottles and seal them tightly to preserve the herbal remedy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi tinctures helps with tinnitus because of its potent adaptogenic properties, which work to balance the nervous system and alleviate stress-related auditory disturbances. The herb's ability to enhance cognitive function, memory, and focus also plays a significant role in calming the mind and reducing mental chatter that can exacerbate tinnitus symptoms. Additionally, brahmi's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the ear, which can contribute to the development of tinnitus. By addressing these underlying factors, brahmi tinctures offer a natural and holistic approach to managing tinnitus.
remedy_properties: Cognitive Enhancing Properties: Brahmi's cognitive-enhancing properties can help to reduce stress, anxiety, and fatigue, which are common contributors to tinnitus, allowing the body to focus on other treatments.

Antioxidant Properties: Brahmi's antioxidant properties can help to protect the hair cells in the inner ear from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can exacerbate tinnitus.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Brahmi's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which is often a underlying cause of tinnitus, thereby alleviating symptoms such as ringing, buzzing, or roaring sounds.
remedy_constituents: Bacosides: Bacosides are a group of triterpenoid saponins found in Bacopa monnieri, which have been shown to have neuroprotective effects and may help to reduce tinnitus by protecting the auditory nerve from damage.

Bacoside A: Bacoside A is a specific bacoside found in Bacopa monnieri, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the auditory system, contributing to the relief of tinnitus symptoms.

Monnierin: Monnierin is a flavonoid glycoside found in Bacopa monnieri, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and may help to reduce the severity of tinnitus by protecting the auditory system from damage and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of bacosides, which are believed to have neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects.
Roots: The roots contain a significant amount of bacosides, which are thought to help alleviate tinnitus symptoms by promoting brain health and reducing inflammation.
Stems: The stems of Bacopa monnieri are also used in tinctures, as they contain a smaller but still notable amount of bacosides, contributing to their potential therapeutic benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried bacopa monnieri roots and 3 parts of fresh bacopa monnieri leaves in late summer.
Chop the harvested bacopa monnieri roots and leaves into small pieces to increase their surface area.
Mix 1 part of chopped bacopa monnieri roots and leaves with 3 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard the solids to obtain a tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn tinctures helps with tinnitus because it has been shown to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the auditory system. The bioactive compounds present in hawthorn, such as flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to alleviate the discomfort and distress associated with tinnitus. Additionally, hawthorn's vasodilatory effects may also help to improve oxygenation of the inner ear, reducing the perceived ringing or buzzing sounds that are characteristic of tinnitus.
remedy_properties: Cardiotonic effects: Hawthorn's cardiotonic properties help to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which can alleviate tinnitus symptoms by reducing damage to hair cells.
Antioxidant activity: Hawthorn's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the inner ear, contributing to tinnitus development.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Hawthorn's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which can help to alleviate symptoms of tinnitus such as ringing, buzzing, or hissing sounds.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin): This flavonoid helps with tinnitus by its anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the ear, potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.

Phenolic Acids (specifically Gallic Acid): Gallic Acid has been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect the auditory system from damage and reduce tinnitus symptoms.

Triterpenes (specifically Ursolic Acid): Ursolic Acid has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can potentially reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the ear, alleviating tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Buds: They are used due to their purported vasodilatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Leaves: They are used because of their potential anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which may help reduce tinnitus-related discomfort.
Fruits: They are used due to their claimed antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms and promote overall ear health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh crataegus monogyna leaves and flowers in the morning when the dew is still present.
Chop 1 part of the harvested material into smaller pieces using a sharp knife for efficient extraction.
Combine the chopped material with 3 parts of 80% ethanol in a clean glass container to create the tincture.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine mesh into another glass container to remove the plant material.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with tinnitus because of its calming and balancing properties. The herbal extract has been shown to reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which can contribute to the development of tinnitus. Additionally, lavender's anxiolytic and sedative effects help to calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate tinnitus symptoms. By promoting relaxation and reducing auditory sensitivity, English lavender tinctures can provide relief from the constant ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: English lavender tincture's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to tinnitus by reducing damage to hair cells in the inner ear.

Anxiolytic and sedative properties: Lavender's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and sedative effects can help alleviate symptoms of tinnitus such as anxiety, stress, and insomnia, which are often comorbid with tinnitus.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which can contribute to tinnitus by damaging or affecting hair cells responsible for sound processing.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the inner ear and alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene has a sedative effect, which may help reduce stress and anxiety, common factors that can exacerbate tinnitus.
Luteolin: This flavonoid has antioxidant properties, which may help protect the inner ear from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, potentially contributing to tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Lavender flowers are commonly used in tinctures due to their calming and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Leaves: Lavender leaves are used in tinctures because of their ability to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, potentially helping to mitigate tinnitus.
Stems: Lavender stems are used in tinctures for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in reducing tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at dawn on a dry day to preserve potency.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm dark place for 2-3 weeks or use a food dehydrator.
Combine the dried flowers with 500ml of vodka in a glass jar and seal it tightly.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks and shake it daily to facilitate infusion.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with tinnitus because of its natural ability to calm and soothe the nervous system. The active compounds in valerian root, such as valerenic acid, work to reduce anxiety and stress levels, which are often linked to tinnitus symptoms. By promoting relaxation and reducing auditory hyperactivity, herbal valerian tinctures may help alleviate the ringing, buzzing, or hissing sounds associated with tinnitus, allowing individuals to experience a more peaceful and quiet environment.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) effects: Valerian's anxiolytic properties can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for tinnitus, allowing individuals to better cope with their symptoms.

Sedative properties: Valerian's sedative effects can help promote relaxation and improve sleep quality, both of which can be disrupted by the constant ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the inner ear, potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: This sesquiterpene acts as a GABA receptor agonist, helping to reduce anxiety and stress, which are often associated with tinnitus.
Isovaleric acid: This short-chain fatty acid has been shown to possess sedative and anticonvulsant properties, which can help alleviate the symptoms of tinnitus, such as ringing or buzzing sounds.
Valerenal: This sesquiterpene acts as a benzodiazepine receptor agonist, producing sedative and anxiolytic effects that can help reduce the severity of tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of valerenic acids, which are believed to contribute to their anxiolytic and sedative effects, potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.

Seeds: Valerian seeds are used for their potential sedative properties, as they contain valerenic acids and other bioactive compounds that may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the valeriana roots and rhizomes in the fall when the plant is dormant for maximum potency and flavor.
Clean the harvested roots and rhizomes thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the cleaned roots and rhizomes into small pieces and combine them with 80 proof vodka in a ratio of 1 part plant material to 3 parts solvent.
Steep the chopped plant material in the vodka for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids to obtain the final tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with tinnitus because of its unique combination of natural compounds that target the underlying causes of this condition. The tincture's flavonoids and saponins work together to reduce inflammation in the auditory nerve, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms. Additionally, maypop's antioxidants help to protect the hair cells in the ear from damage, allowing them to function properly and reducing the ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus. By addressing these underlying causes, maypop tinctures offer a natural solution for managing tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Maypop contains flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the ear, potentially alleviating Tinnitus symptoms.
Tannins: Maypop's high concentration of tannins can help constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling in the ears, which may relieve pressure and discomfort associated with Tinnitus.

Phenolic Acids: The phenolic acids present in Maypop have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may help protect against ear damage and promote healing in individuals with Tinnitus.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Passiflora incarnata, particularly kaempferol and quercetin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the inner ear, which may contribute to tinnitus.
Harman alkaloids: Harman alkaloids, such as harmine and harmaline, have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate anxiety and stress that may exacerbate tinnitus.

Isoflavones: Isoflavones, including vitexin and orientin, have been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system and may also have a direct effect on the auditory system, potentially reducing the perception of tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used in tinctures for tinnitus due to their high concentration of flavonoids and alkaloids, which are believed to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Utilized in tinctures to take advantage of their sedative and antispasmodic properties, which may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Fruits: Employed in tinctures due to their content of flavonoids, vitamins, and minerals, which may contribute to their potential therapeutic effects on tinnitus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves in the early morning, when the plant is at its highest potency level.
Clean and dry the harvested passiflora incarnata parts thoroughly using a clean cloth to prevent contamination.
Combine 1 part passiflora incarnata with 2 parts vodka or 80 proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng tinctures helps with tinnitus because of its ability to improve blood flow to the inner ear, which can help reduce ringing and buzzing sounds associated with the condition. The antioxidant properties in ginseng also help protect the delicate hair cells in the ear from damage, potentially reducing the intensity of tinnitus symptoms. Additionally, ginseng has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which may help alleviate stress and anxiety that can exacerbate tinnitus symptoms, providing relief for those affected by this condition.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic and Anti-Stress Properties: Ginseng tinctures have been shown to reduce stress and anxiety levels, which are often linked to tinnitus symptoms. By reducing stress, ginseng may help alleviate tinnitus-related discomfort.

Antioxidant Activity: Ginseng's antioxidant properties help protect the inner ear from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to tinnitus development and progression. By neutralizing these damaging compounds, ginseng may help reduce tinnitus symptoms.

Neuroprotective Properties: Ginseng has been found to possess neuroprotective compounds that may help prevent or slow down the progression of hearing loss and tinnitus-related auditory damage. This property may help preserve hearing function and alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These compounds, particularly ginsenoside Rb1, have been found to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the ear, contributing to tinnitus relief.

Glycosides: The glycoside content in Panax ginseng, such as ginsenoside Rd, has been shown to have anti-apoptotic effects, which may help protect auditory neurons from damage and reduce the risk of tinnitus.
Saponins: Saponins in Panax ginseng, including ginsenoside Re, have been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the ear and alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part of Panax ginseng for tinctures due to their high concentration of ginsenosides, which are believed to have neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for tinnitus treatment.
Stems: The stems contain smaller amounts of ginsenosides compared to the roots but are still used in some tincture formulations due to their potential synergy with the root's active compounds.
Barks: The barks of the Panax ginseng plant may be used in some tinctures to add additional active compounds and enhance the overall efficacy of the treatment for tinnitus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of panax ginseng roots in late summer or early fall when the plant is mature.
Clean and chop the ginseng roots into smaller pieces to increase their surface area.
Steep 1 part of ginseng roots in 2-3 parts of 80-100 proof vodka or ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to infuse in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with tinnitus because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce the inflammation and oxidative stress in the inner ear that may be contributing to the ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus. The bioactive compounds in licorice root, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have been shown to possess potent protective effects on the auditory system, making them a potential natural remedy for managing this condition.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the ear and surrounding tissues, often associated with tinnitus.

Flavonoids: These bioactive compounds have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the inner ear from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, potentially contributing to tinnitus symptoms.

Triterpenoid saponins: These molecules have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help reduce infections or inflammation in the middle ear that could be causing or exacerbating tinnitus.
remedy_constituents: Licoricidin: Licoricidin has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which can contribute to tinnitus.

Licorice saponins (Glycyrrhizin): Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help protect the auditory system from damage and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to tinnitus.
Flavonoids (Licoagroside and Iso-licagroside): Flavonoids have antioxidant properties that help protect the auditory system from damage and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to make tinctures for tinnitus because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate symptoms.
Roots: They are also used to make tinctures for tinnitus because they contain flavonoids and saponins, which may help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the auditory system.

Roots: Additionally, they are used to make tinctures for tinnitus because they have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to soothe and calm the nervous system, which may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root and 2 parts of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the root in the ethanol at room temperature for 2 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Transfer the liquid to a clean glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place for 2 weeks.
Filter the liquid through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle for final storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with tinnitus because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive compound curcumin in turmeric has been shown to reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the development of tinnitus. By reducing inflammation in the auditory system, turmeric tinctures may help alleviate symptoms such as ringing, buzzing, or hissing sounds that are commonly associated with tinnitus. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of curcumin can protect against oxidative stress and damage to the auditory hair cells, which can further contribute to improved tinnitus management.
remedy_properties: Curcumin's Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Turmeric contains a powerful anti-inflammatory compound called curcumin, which can help reduce inflammation in the inner ear and auditory nerve, potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.
Turmeric's Antioxidant Activity: Turmeric's antioxidant properties can help protect the inner ear from damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to tinnitus development and progression.
Curcumin's Neuroprotective Effects: Curcumin has been shown to have neuroprotective effects, which may help prevent or slow down auditory nerve damage that can contribute to tinnitus.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: A polyphenolic compound that helps with tinnitus by reducing inflammation in the auditory nerve and surrounding tissues, which may alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): A curcuminoid with antioxidant properties that may help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation in the auditory system, contributing to tinnitus relief.

Ar-turmerone: A sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with tinnitus, potentially leading to symptom improvement.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: These are the underground stems of the plant that contain a high concentration of curcumin, a compound that may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa are also a source of curcumin and other bioactive compounds, which may contribute to their potential therapeutic effects in tinnitus treatment.

Barks: The barks of the plant are sometimes used to make tinctures due to their alleged anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help mitigate tinnitus-related inflammation and oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh curcuma longa roots in the morning when the dew is still present on the surface.
Clean the harvested roots by gently scrubbing with a soft brush to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and combine with 80% ethanol at a 1:5 ratio to create a tincture.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai tinctures helps with tinnitus because of its ability to promote blood flow and balance to the inner ear. As a natural hormone regulator, dong quai tincture can also help to normalize hormonal imbalances that may be contributing to tinnitus symptoms. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties can soothe and calm the nervous system, reducing inflammation in the ear that can exacerbate ringing or buzzing sounds. remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried angelica sinensis root and vodka or grain alcohol of at least 70% abv.
Combine one part dried angelica sinensis root with three parts vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar every day to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and herbs.
Store the finished angelica sinensis tincture in a cool dark place at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha tinctures helps with tinnitus because of its adaptogenic properties, which enable it to calm the nervous system and reduce stress levels. This calming effect can help alleviate the emotional anxiety that often accompanies tinnitus, allowing individuals to focus on other sounds rather than the ringing or buzzing in their ears. Additionally, ashwagandha's anti-inflammatory properties may help soothe and protect the auditory system, potentially reducing the severity of tinnitus symptoms. As a result, many people have reported improved quality of life and reduced tinnitus symptoms after incorporating ashwagandha tinctures into their daily routine.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: Ashwagandha's adaptogenic properties help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for tinnitus, by regulating the body's response to stress hormones like cortisol.

Antioxidant Activity: Ashwagandha's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage hair cells in the inner ear and contribute to tinnitus symptoms, helping to protect hearing and alleviate symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory Effects: Ashwagandha's anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation in the body, which has been linked to tinnitus, by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules like nitric oxide.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Ashwagandha plant contain a high concentration of withanolides, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate Tinnitus symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves are rich in chlorophyll and other bioactive compounds that may help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the ear, potentially alleviating Tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality withania somnfera roots and clean them thoroughly.
Cut the cleaned roots into small pieces and dry them in a well-ventilated area for two weeks.
Grind 100 grams of dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered root with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar and let it steep for six weeks.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and store it in dark glass bottles at room temperature for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with tinnitus because of its unique ability to soothe and calm the auditory system. The menthol in peppermint oil has a profound effect on reducing inflammation and irritation in the ears, which is often a root cause of tinnitus symptoms. Additionally, peppermint's natural analgesic properties help to reduce discomfort and tension associated with tinnitus, allowing individuals to better cope with its effects. By promoting relaxation and alleviating discomfort, herbal peppermint tinctures provide a natural and effective way to manage the debilitating symptoms of tinnitus.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Peppermint tincture's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which is often linked to tinnitus symptoms.
Pain relief and analgesic properties: Peppermint's pain-relieving compounds can help alleviate the discomfort and pressure associated with tinnitus, allowing for improved quality of life.

Vaso-dilatory effects: Peppermint tincture's ability to dilate blood vessels may help improve circulation to the inner ear, which could potentially reduce or eliminate tinnitus symptoms by increasing oxygen supply to the affected area.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant contain a high concentration of menthol, which has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.

Barks: The barks of the peppermint plant contain flavonoids, such as quercetin, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the ear, potentially alleviating tinnitus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from a healthy plant, selecting those with no signs of disease or pests, for optimal quality and potency.
Clean and chop the harvested peppermint leaves into small pieces, removing any stems or damaged areas, to ensure maximum extraction efficiency.
Combine 20-30 grams of chopped peppermint leaves with 200ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, sealing it tightly to prevent contamination.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it daily, allowing the tincture to extract and mature slowly.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids, and store the finished tincture in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with tinnitus because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The Echinacea species, native to the Great Plains region, has been traditionally used to soothe earaches and reduce inflammation in the auditory system. The tincture's active compounds, such as alkylamides and polyphenols, may help to reduce inner ear inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to tinnitus symptoms. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy ear function, Kansas coneflower tinctures may provide relief from the ringing or buzzing associated with tinnitus.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas Coneflower's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, can help reduce inflammation in the inner ear and auditory nerve, which is thought to contribute to tinnitus symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds present in Kansas Coneflower, including phenolic acids and flavonoids, can help protect against oxidative stress and damage to the ear's delicate structures, which may be contributing to tinnitus.

Antimicrobial properties: Kansas Coneflower's antimicrobial compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones and alkaloids, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms in the ear canal, which can contribute to tinnitus by causing irritation or infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in soothing tinnitus symptoms.
Fruits: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, these parts help alleviate ear inflammation and reduce oxidative stress, contributing to relief from tinnitus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea angustifolia flowers and leaves in the morning.
Chop the plant material into small pieces using scissors or a sharp knife.
Combine the chopped plant material with 100 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place and let it steep for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew tinctures helps with tinnitus because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation and relax the auditory system. The botanical compound parthenolide, found in feverfew, has been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which can cause ear irritation and exacerbate tinnitus symptoms. Additionally, feverfew's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for tinnitus. By calming the nerves and soothing the ears, feverfew tinctures provide relief from the constant ringing or buzzing associated with this condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Feverfew's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as parthenolide, may help reduce inflammation in the ear and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to tinnitus.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in feverfew, including flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, can help protect the inner ear from oxidative stress and damage, which may exacerbate tinnitus symptoms.

Antispasmodic properties: Feverfew's antispasmodic compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones, may help relax muscle spasms in the ear and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to tinnitus ringing or buzzing sensations.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the inner ear, making them an effective treatment for tinnitus.
Leaves: Feverfew leaves contain parthenolide, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, helping to alleviate symptoms of tinnitus such as ringing or buzzing in the ears.
remedy_recipe: Gather 200g of dried tanacetum parthenium flowers and transfer them to a clean glass jar.
Pour 500ml of high-proof vodka over the dried flowers in the jar, ensuring they are completely covered.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking it daily to facilitate extraction.
After 14 days, strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Label and store the resulting tincture at room temperature, allowing it to mature for at least 30 days before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Roselle tinctures helps with tinnitus because of its ability to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that often exacerbate tinnitus symptoms. The tannins and antioxidants present in roselle have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the ears, which is a common cause of tinnitus. Additionally, the herb's flavonoids and phenolic acids work together to improve blood flow to the ears, promoting healthy ear function and reducing ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus. By addressing these underlying causes, roselle tinctures can provide effective relief from tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Roselle tincture's anti-inflammatory effects can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the inner ear, which is often associated with tinnitus.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Roselle tincture can help protect the cochlea (the spiral-shaped structure responsible for hearing) from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to tinnitus development.

Stress-relieving and anxiolytic effects: Roselle tincture's calming and sedative properties can help reduce anxiety and stress, which are common exacerbating factors of tinnitus, and promote relaxation and improved sleep quality.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Roselle plant contain bioactive compounds that help to reduce tinnitus symptoms by calming the nervous system and promoting relaxation.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of the Roselle plant have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate tinnitus symptoms by reducing inflammation in the ear and auditory nerve.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried hibiscus sabdariffa flowers and leaves for optimal potency and flavor.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine chopped plant material with 70% grain alcohol in a clean glass jar covering the mixture completely.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks shaking the jar daily allowing the plant's active compounds to infuse into the solvent.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and label with date and ingredients used.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with tinnitus because of its unique combination of bioactive compounds, which have been shown to have a calming effect on the auditory system. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in elder tincture help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the inner ear, which is often associated with tinnitus symptoms. Additionally, the natural relaxants in elder tincture can help alleviate anxiety and stress-related tinnitus, allowing individuals to find relief from this debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Elder tincture's high antioxidant content helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the ear, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms.
Vasodilatory effects: The flavonoids present in elder tincture may help to dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow to the ears and reducing pressure on the cochlea, a common cause of tinnitus.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the ear, which can exacerbate tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots have been traditionally used to treat ear problems, including tinnitus, due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: Elder barks contain compounds that have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate symptoms of tinnitus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Weigh and combine one part of the harvested material with two parts of vodka or high-proof spirit.
Mix the botanicals and solvent in a clean glass jar, ensuring all particles are fully saturated.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking daily, allowing extraction time.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, discard solids, and transfer tincture to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock tinctures helps with tinnitus because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation in the inner ear, a common cause of tinnitus. The bioactive compounds in burdock, such as lignans and polyphenols, also have antioxidant effects that help protect the delicate hair cells in the cochlea from damage, which can contribute to the perception of ringing or buzzing sounds. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal burdock tinctures may help alleviate the symptoms of tinnitus and promote a sense of inner ear balance and function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Burdock root has anti-inflammatory compounds like sesquiterpenes, which may help reduce inflammation in the inner ear and alleviate tinnitus symptoms by reducing noise sensitivity.

Antioxidant: Burdock tincture's antioxidant properties may help protect the inner ear from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to tinnitus development or exacerbation.

remedy_parts: Roots: Burdock roots contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the ears, making it effective for treating tinnitus.
Seeds: The seeds of the burdock plant have been traditionally used to treat ear infections and hearing problems, including tinnitus, due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried arctium lappa roots and chop them into small pieces for easy infusion.
Combine one part chopped roots with five parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar and seal tightly.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking the jar every other day to enhance extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the tincture.
Transfer the resulting tincture to smaller glass bottles and label them with dosage instructions and preparation information for future use.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to tinnitus that are treated with herbal tinctures are conditions affecting the ear, nose, and throat, such as otitis media (middle ear inflammation), tonsillitis, and laryngitis. Herbal tinctures like Gelsemium sempervirens can help alleviate symptoms of hyperacusis, a condition characterized by an increased sensitivity to sound. Other herbs like Calendula officinalis and Echinacea angustifolia may aid in soothing ear infections and promoting overall auditory health.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with tinnitus is a blend of Ginkgo biloba, St. John's Wort, and passionflower. Ginkgo biloba improves blood flow to the ears, while St. John's Wort helps reduce inflammation and anxiety-related symptoms. Passionflower calms the nervous system, reducing stress and promoting relaxation. This combination can be taken in small doses, 2-3 times a day, under the guidance of a healthcare professional. Regular use may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms and promote overall auditory health.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tinctures helps with tinnitus because it is believed to improve blood flow to the ears, reducing the inflammation and damage caused by poor circulation. The antioxidant properties of ginkgo biloba may also help protect the auditory nerve from oxidative stress, which can contribute to tinnitus. Additionally, ginkgo biloba's ability to modulate neurotransmitters may help regulate the auditory system, reducing the perception of ringing or other sounds associated with tinnitus. This may lead to improved hearing and reduced tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help alleviate tinnitus by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the auditory system, which may contribute to the development of tinnitus.

Terpenoids (specifically Bilobalide): Bilobalide helps reduce tinnitus symptoms by improving blood flow to the inner ear, reducing oxidative stress, and potentially repairing damaged auditory nerve cells.

Ginkgolides (specifically Ginkgolide B): Ginkgolide B has anti-inflammatory properties and helps block platelet-activating factor (PAF), a chemical involved in the development of tinnitus and other inflammatory conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to improve blood flow to the inner ear and reduce inflammation associated with tinnitus.
Seeds: They are a rich source of ginkgotoxins, which may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms by improving blood circulation and reducing oxidative stress.
Barks: They contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which may help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the inner ear, potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part ginkgo biloba leaves and 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the ginkgo biloba leaves in vodka for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid from the jar using cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Transfer the liquid to a clean glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place.
After 2 months, use the tincture as needed by taking 20-30 drops 2-3 times daily.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures helps with tinnitus because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the auditory system. Ginger's bioactive compounds, particularly gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to exhibit neuroprotective effects, which may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms by protecting the auditory nerve and reducing oxidative stress. Additionally, ginger's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate ear pressure and discomfort associated with tinnitus, promoting a sense of relief and balance. This may contribute to improved quality of life for individuals suffering from tinnitus.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms by reducing inflammation in the ear and surrounding tissues.
Gingerdienes: Gingerdienes have been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can protect the ear from oxidative stress and potentially reduce tinnitus symptoms.
Shogaols: Shogaols have been found to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms by reducing inflammation and pain associated with the condition.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They contain bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Roots: They are rich in essential oils, which are often used in tinctures to help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the ears.
Buds: They contain high concentrations of bioactive compounds, which are extracted and used in tinctures to help alleviate tinnitus symptoms due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500g of fresh zingiber officinale roots and clean them thoroughly with water.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and set aside for 2 hours to dry.
Combine the dried root pieces with 750ml of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 weeks to allow steeping and infusion.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa tinctures helps with tinnitus because it is believed to promote relaxation and reduce stress, two common triggers for tinnitus. The tincture's calming properties can help alleviate anxiety and promote a sense of balance, which can contribute to a decrease in tinnitus symptoms. Additionally, Avena sativa has been traditionally used to support the nervous system, which may help to regulate and stabilize the auditory system, providing relief from tinnitus. This natural approach can be a valuable adjunct to conventional treatments.
remedy_constituents: Silicic acid: Helps with tinnitus by its potential anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on the inner ear, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Avenacoside: May help with tinnitus by its antioxidant properties, which can protect the inner ear from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals.
Fagopyritin: Could aid in tinnitus relief by its potential ability to regulate and stabilize the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that may contribute to tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Avena sativa are commonly used to make tinctures for tinnitus due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds that may help alleviate symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves are a popular choice for tincture production because they contain flavonoids and other polyphenolic compounds that may help reduce tinnitus symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Avena sativa are often used in tinctures for tinnitus due to their rich content of saponins, which may have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried avena sativa roots and 3 parts of fresh avena sativa leaves in a clean bucket.
Combine the harvested avena sativa roots and leaves with 70 proof vodka in a 1:4 ratio by volume.
Seal the bucket and steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and filter the liquid.
Label and date the bottle and store the avena sativa tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis tinctures helps with tinnitus because of its unique properties that promote relaxation and calmness in the nervous system. The herb's natural anxiolytic and anti-inflammatory effects help to reduce stress and inflammation in the auditory system, which is often associated with tinnitus. remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound helps with tinnitus by potentially reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the ear, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms.
Linalool: As a terpene, linalool has anti-anxiety and sedative effects, which can help alleviate stress and anxiety-related tinnitus.
Melissic acid: This phenolic compound has been shown to possess antioxidant properties, which can help protect the ear from damage and reduce tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of essential oils and rosmarinic acid, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their soothing and calming effects, helping to reduce anxiety and stress that can contribute to tinnitus.
Roots: The roots are sometimes used to add depth and grounding to the tincture, as they contain a higher concentration of alkaloids and flavonoids compared to the leaves and flowers.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part melissa officinalis flowers and 2 parts vegetable glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Steep the herb mixture in the glycerin for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the solid particles.
Transfer the resulting liquid to a smaller glass bottle and store in a cool dark place.
Label and date the bottle with the ingredients and preparation instructions for future reference.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tinctures helps with tinnitus because of its unique combination of chemical compounds, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system. These compounds can help alleviate stress and anxiety, common triggers of tinnitus. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of lavender may help reduce inflammation in the ear, a potential cause of tinnitus. Linalyl acetate: As a terpene, linalyl acetate has been shown to possess sedative and anxiolytic effects, which can help alleviate stress and anxiety associated with tinnitus.

Caffeic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant properties that may help protect the auditory system from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, potentially reducing tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part of Lavandula angustifolia in tinctures for tinnitus due to their high concentration of essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and anxiolytic effects.
Leaves: The leaves are used in tinctures for tinnitus as they contain similar essential oils to the flowers, providing a complementary effect in reducing anxiety and inflammation.

Roots: The roots are also used in tinctures for tinnitus as they contain a higher concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest lavandula angustifolia flowers on a dry sunny morning in late summer to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the freshly harvested flowers in a single layer on paper bags or screens for 2 weeks.
Combine 1 part dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and shake it gently every day for 2 weeks to facilitate infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container to separate the tincture from the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Bacopa monnieri tinctures helps with tinnitus because it is believed to improve cognitive function and reduce stress, which are often associated with the condition. The herb is thought to enhance memory and concentration by promoting the growth of new neurons and improving communication between brain cells. By reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, Bacopa monnieri may also help alleviate tinnitus symptoms. Additionally, its potential to regulate neurotransmitters and calm the nervous system may help mitigate tinnitus-related anxiety and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Bacosides: These saponin glycosides help with tinnitus by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the ear, which can contribute to tinnitus.
Bacopaside II: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate tinnitus symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting ear health.
Bacoside A: This triterpenoid saponin has antioxidant properties, which can help protect the ear from oxidative stress and damage, potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain the highest concentration of bacosides, which are the active compounds responsible for the herb's medicinal properties.
Stems: The stems of Bacopa monnieri contain a significant amount of bacosides, making them a popular choice for tinctures.
Roots: The roots of Bacopa monnieri are also used for their bacoside content, although in smaller quantities compared to the leaves and stems.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried bacopa monnieri leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated with a ratio of 1:2.
Combine the harvested plant material with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar with a ratio of 1:2.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Transfer the herbal tincture to dark glass dropper bottles and store it in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum tinctures helps with tinnitus because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herbal remedy, also known as St. John's Wort, is believed to improve blood circulation and reduce oxidative stress in the ear, which may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms. Additionally, Hypericum perforatum is thought to have a positive effect on neurotransmitters and hormones that regulate hearing and balance, potentially reducing the ringing or buzzing associated with tinnitus. remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: It helps reduce inflammation in the auditory system, which is often associated with tinnitus, and also has antioxidant properties to protect the auditory nerve.
Quercetin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the inflammation of the auditory system and alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Kaempferol: It has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect the auditory nerve and reduce inflammation in the auditory system, contributing to relief from tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in hypericin and hyperforin, which may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms by their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and volatile oils that may contribute to the tinnitus-relieving effects of Hypericum perforatum tinctures.
Roots: May be used in tinnitus tinctures due to their content of flavonoids, which have been shown to have potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of the aerial parts of the plant in the morning when the dew is still present.
Dry the harvested plant material at room temperature for 1-2 weeks in a well-ventilated area.
Combine 1 part of the dried plant material with 2 parts of 60% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking every day.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Scutellaria baicalensis tinctures helps with tinnitus because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tincture, made from the roots of the Baikal skullcap plant, has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various ailments, including ear and hearing issues. Research suggests that its flavonoids and phenolic acids may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the auditory system, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms. remedy_constituents: Baicalein: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the ear and alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Baicalin: A flavonoid glycoside, baicalin exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help protect the ear from damage and reduce tinnitus.
Wogonin: This flavone has anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the ear, leading to relief from tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high content of baicalin, a flavonoid compound believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: They contain a range of flavonoids and phenolic acids that may help in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with tinnitus.
Flowers: They are used for their flavonoid content, particularly baicalin, which is thought to contribute to their potential therapeutic effects in treating tinnitus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried scutellaria baicalensis root and rinse it with cold water to remove impurities.
Chop the dried root into small pieces and combine it with 2 cups of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to small dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata tinctures helps with tinnitus because it contains sedative properties that promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, common underlying causes of tinnitus. remedy_constituents: Harmane: Acts as an anxiolytic agent, which can help reduce stress-induced tinnitus symptoms.
Isovitexin: Has flavonoid properties that may help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation in the inner ear, contributing to tinnitus relief.
Flavonoids: May help reduce inflammation and promote neuroprotection in the auditory pathways, potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of passifloric acid and flavonoids, which are believed to have anxiolytic and anticonvulsant properties that may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Fruits: Fruits are used for their high content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with tinnitus.
Barks: Barks are utilized for their potential analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with tinnitus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves in the early morning on a dry day.
Clean the harvested passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove any dirt.
Combine 1-2 cups of the cleaned passiflora incarnata with 2 cups of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar every day or two.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis tinctures helps with tinnitus because of its potential to reduce inflammation and improve circulation in the ears. Sigmasterol: This triterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help protect the inner ear from damage and reduce tinnitus symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried angelica sinensis roots and 2 parts of dried angelica sinensis leaves.
Chop the harvested angelica sinensis roots and leaves into small pieces to increase surface area.
Combine 1 part of chopped angelica sinensis with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a dark cool place for 2 to 4 weeks to allow the tincture to mature.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.
